prevalence.source: Indian child development studies + WHO context

incidence.value: 2-5 per 1,000 live births (diagnosed over early childhood)

vulnerablePopulations: children in rural areas with limited diagnostic services
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11 ASD criteria; diagnosis often clinical with standardized tools in tertiary centers

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: low; developmental screening not routine in many settings
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Not applicable; outcomes depend on early intervention access

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Diagnosis concentrated in urban developmental pediatrics/psychiatry; rural families face delays
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Early intervention, speech/occupational therapy, special education supports; caregiver training; comorbidity management
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Underdiagnosis, limited therapy workforce, high out-of-pocket costs, and variable school inclusion resources
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Growing awareness and expanding private/NGO early intervention services in cities
